regions.us-ports: Today, Charleston is experiencing light SSW winds at 4 knots with clear skies and zero percent chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions with no weather disruptions expected. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, ports including Charleston, Savannah, and Jacksonville will see increasing S to SSW winds up to 12 knots, with skies turning overcast but still no rain, which may slightly reduce visibility but should not impact port operations. By day three, Charleston and neighboring Gulf and Atlantic ports such as Galveston and Tampa Bay will continue with SSW winds near 11 knots and overcast skies, maintaining dry conditions favorable for cruise departures and arrivals. Passengers and crew at northern ports including Boston, Manhattan, and Bayonne should also anticipate mostly clear conditions today with light winds, shifting to more overcast skies later in the period without precipitation. No marine wind warnings or severe weather threats are present or forecast within this timeframe, allowing for normal vessel maneuvering and safe shore activity.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, conditions across Nassau and adjacent Bahamas ports including Freeport, Bimini, and the Berry Islands feature northeast winds near 6 knots with overcast skies and zero percent rain chance, allowing normal embarkation and shore excursions with minimal weather disruption. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect northeast winds to increase to 11-15 knots in Nassau and nearby ports, maintaining overcast skies with only a 2-4 percent chance of rain, which should keep port operations stable in Key West, Grand Cayman, and Ocho Rios as well. By the third day, winds will shift slightly east-northeast at 15 knots with drizzle expected and a 4 percent rain chance, which may result in damp conditions during disembarkation or shore excursions at Falmouth, Puerto Plata, La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná. No small craft advisories or severe weather warnings are forecasted, so cruise operations should continue smoothly, though drizzle might require some shelter during outdoor activities beyond 48 hours. Passengers and crew should monitor for any updates as the east-northeast wind and light precipitation trend develops through the midweek period.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, rain showers with a 100% probability will impact port operations and shore excursions primarily in San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, and St. Maarten as easterly winds increase to 7 knots and skies remain mostly cloudy. Expect delays in embarkation and disembarkation at these ports due to wet conditions and reduced visibility caused by steady rain under east winds. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, thunderstorms with an 85% rain probability will affect Antigua, St. Kitts, and St. Maarten, accompanied by east winds rising to 10 knots, which may disrupt tender operations and outdoor excursions. Beyond 48 hours, thunderstorms with a 72% chance of rain will continue across San Juan and St. Thomas with east winds strengthening to 12 knots, suggesting sustained unsettled weather that could impact ship maneuvers and port activities. Crew and passengers should prepare for reduced visibility and wet decks during these periods, adjusting schedules accordingly.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, winds out of the east at 9 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 0% chance of rain will support smooth port operations and shore excursions in Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, east winds will increase slightly to 11-13 knots with continued partly cloudy conditions and minimal rain probabilities between 1 and 3 percent, maintaining favorable conditions for embarkation and disembarkation at all four ports. By day three, winds will shift modestly to east-northeast at 10 knots with a low 3% chance of rain, which may cause minor changes in sea state near Roatan but should not disrupt cruise schedules. No small craft advisories or tropical weather systems are forecast in the region during this period, so passenger and crew operations can proceed without weather-related delays. We will continue monitoring for any shifts in wind direction or rain chances beyond 48 hours that could affect sea conditions or port access.
regions.southern-caribbean: PRIORITY ALERT – Significant weather conditions are forecast for Day 2 affecting Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena with rain showers and a 35% chance of rain. Today in Aruba, we have ENE winds at 10 knots with partly cloudy skies and only a 5% chance of rain, supporting normal port operations and shore excursions with minimal disruption. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing ENE winds up to 14 knots and rain showers with a 35% rain probability in Aruba, with similar conditions likely impacting Curacao and Bonaire, which may cause delays or adjustments to embarkation, disembarkation, and outdoor excursions. By Day 3, winds remain steady at ENE 13 knots with overcast skies and a 33% chance of rain extending to Cartagena, requiring heightened awareness for vessel maneuvering and passenger safety during port calls. Please continue monitoring updates as these conditions develop to ensure smooth cruise operations and passenger comfort.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today expect easterly winds near 14 knots with overcast skies and only an 8 percent chance of rain in Barbados, allowing mostly smooth port operations and shore excursions there. However, rain showers with a 40 percent probability and east winds increasing to 17 knots will affect St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, and Grenada, potentially causing intermittent wet conditions and minor delays in embarkation or outdoor activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, the eastern Caribbean including Antigua, St. Kitts, and St. Maarten will see sustained east winds up to 18 knots with overcast skies and a 25 percent chance of rain, which may lead to damp conditions but generally manageable port activity. By 48 to 72 hours, expect rain with an 8 percent probability and steady east winds near 17 knots in Barbados and nearby islands, signaling a slight reduction in rain but continued overcast conditions that could limit visibility for navigation and outdoor excursions. All vessels and shore teams should monitor these evolving wind speeds and rain chances closely to adjust schedules and ensure passenger safety.
regions.los-angeles: Today, winds out of the west at 6 knots combined with clear skies and zero percent rain probability will support smooth port operations and seamless embarkation activities at Los Angeles / San Pedro, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island. Visibility remains excellent with no precipitation expected, so shore excursions will proceed without weather disruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect westerly winds to increase to 11 knots with overcast skies at Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach, while Long Beach and Marina del Rey will see southerly winds at 8 knots and continued overcast conditions; all ports will remain dry with zero percent rain chance, ensuring continued operational stability. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift back to the west at 12 knots accompanied by partly cloudy skies and no rain, maintaining favorable conditions for cruise departures and arrivals across all Southern California ports. No wind warnings or marine advisories are in effect, so cruise vessels should anticipate steady, manageable sea states throughout this period.
regions.ensenada: Today in the Ensenada region, winds are light from the northwest at 1 knot with clear skies and zero percent chance of rain, providing excellent conditions for smooth port operations and uninterrupted shore excursions at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing westerly to southwesterly winds between 9 and 12 knots with overcast skies and no rain at Ensenada and Punta Banda, which may slightly reduce visibility but should not impact vessel maneuvering or guest activities. By day three, southwest winds will hold steady near 10 knots with continued overcast conditions and a minimal 2 percent chance of rain, primarily affecting Islas Todos Santos, where excursion operators should monitor for any changes. No advisories or marine warnings are in effect, so cruise operations and guest embarkation/disembarkation will proceed without weather-related delays throughout the period. Passengers and crew should remain aware of the gradual increase in wind speed and cloud cover but can anticipate generally safe and manageable weather conditions in all ports.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: PRIORITY ALERT – Elevated winds reaching 22 knots are forecast for Day 3, impacting Cabo San Lucas and San Jose del Cabo ports. Today, winds are NNW at 13 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero rain chance, allowing normal embarkation and shore excursions in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect NNW winds increasing to 18 knots with overcast skies and no rain, which may cause moderate choppiness affecting tender operations primarily in Cabo San Lucas and San Jose del Cabo. Beyond 48 hours, sustained NNW winds will rise to 22 knots with overcast conditions, triggering a Small Craft Advisory level wind alert at Cabo San Lucas and San Jose del Cabo, likely causing adjustments to ship maneuvering and shore excursion scheduling. La Paz will remain less affected with lighter winds and partly cloudy skies through the 3-day outlook.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, winds are NNW at 6 knots with overcast skies and a 0% chance of rain, providing smooth conditions for embarkation and shore excursions at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing NW winds up to 16 knots today and 13 knots tomorrow with persistent overcast skies and no rain, which will maintain steady sea states and minimal disruption to port operations across all three ports. Beyond 48 hours, NW winds will hold near 13 knots with continued overcast conditions and zero rain probability, supporting stable cruise ship maneuvering and safe tendering activities. Passengers and crew should anticipate consistent wind directions and cloud cover, with no weather-related delays expected during this period.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today in Puerto Vallarta, we have light easterly winds at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent chance of rain, which supports smooth port operations and no disruptions for shore excursions at Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, or Chacala. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift to the west at 7 knots on day one and then to west-northwest at 7 knots on day two, with overcast skies and no rain expected, so all four ports should continue normal embarkation and disembarkation activities without weather delays. Beyond 48 hours, winds will veer northwest at 8 knots while skies remain overcast and rain chances stay at zero percent, indicating consistent conditions for cruise ship maneuvering and passenger safety at all ports in the region. No wind speeds approach Small Craft Advisory thresholds, so sea state concerns remain minimal for vessel transit and tender operations throughout this period. Passengers and crew should expect steady but light winds and stable visibility under predominantly cloudy skies for the next three days.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today, conditions in Barcelona feature clear skies with light northwest winds at 2 knots and zero percent chance of rain, allowing for smooth embarkation and disembarkation with no expected delays to shore excursions. Ports across the Western Mediterranean including Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, and Ibiza will see similar favorable conditions with light winds and dry skies, supporting uninterrupted port operations. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift to a southerly direction at 7 knots with partly cloudy skies and no rain expected, which may slightly increase sea state but will not impact docking procedures at Malaga, Cadiz, or Gibraltar. Beyond 48 hours, these SSW winds at 7 knots and partly cloudy conditions are forecast to persist, maintaining stable operational conditions for Lisbon and surrounding ports with no marine warnings or precipitation expected. Passengers and crew can plan shore activities and vessel movements confidently with these consistent light wind and dry weather conditions throughout the region.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today, a high-impact weather event is underway with thunderstorms and a 93% rain probability forecast for Naples and nearby ports such as Civitavecchia and La Spezia, where NNE winds at 6 to 7 knots will challenge embarkation and shore excursions due to wet conditions and reduced visibility under overcast skies. Current conditions at Naples show lighter NE winds at 5 knots with only a 25% rain chance, but this will quickly escalate to heavy thunderstorms, necessitating heightened operational caution and possible delays in Naples and southern ports including Naples and Livorno. Over the next 24-48 hours, expect improving conditions with overcast skies and rain chances dropping to 3-5% as N to NNE winds steady around 6-7 knots, benefiting ports like Genoa, Monaco, and Nice with safer and smoother port operations. Beyond 48 hours, the Central Mediterranean will maintain overcast skies and light winds from the north at 6 knots with minimal rain, allowing for resumed normal cruise schedules and shore excursions across Marseille, Corsica, and Sardinia. Passengers and crew should remain alert for localized thunderstorms today in southern ports and anticipate improving conditions toward midweek.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today in the Eastern Mediterranean, conditions at Athens (Piraeus) feature light northwest winds at 3 knots with overcast skies and only a 3% chance of rain, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions to proceed without weather-related disruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing northerly winds up to 7 knots from NNE to N and persistent overcast conditions with rain probabilities peaking at 25% on Day 1, which may cause minor delays or adjustments for ports like Santorini, Mykonos, and Izmir. By Day 3, winds shift to NNW at 8 knots with a reduced 3% rain chance but continued overcast skies, suggesting improved conditions for sailing toward Rhodes, Cyprus, and Alexandria. Passengers and crew should monitor these gradual wind shifts and cloud cover trends as they may affect visibility during embarkation and disembarkation at Istanbul and Haifa beyond 48 hours. Overall, while no significant marine wind warnings are in effect, cruise operations should remain alert to the moderate northerly breeze and occasional light rain impacting scheduling and outdoor activities.
